scope: Protocol 91 client-message parser grammar for docs/plans/2026-06-05-networking-2.md

client_message_body.relative_to: current msg cursor after Netchan_Process and SV_Netchan_Decode

fields.name: retailClientMessageSideband
fields.validation: retail server consumes but does not validate this byte before opcode parsing

parse_flow: CL_WritePacket writes serverId, serverMessageSequence, serverCommandSequence, and the retail sideband byte.
CL_WritePacket then writes zero or more clc_clientCommand payloads followed by clc_move or clc_moveNoDelta when commands are available.
CL_Netchan_Transmit appends clc_EOF and CL_Netchan_Encode XORs from CL_ENCODE_START 12, including the sideband byte.
SV_Netchan_Decode reads the three clear header longs and decodes from SV_DECODE_START 12, including the sideband byte.
SV_ExecuteClientMessage reads serverId, messageAcknowledge, reliableAcknowledge, consumes the sideband byte, then starts opcode parsing at offset 13.

expected.notes: A Quake III-style body without the sideband would have put clc_move at offset 12; the retail parser consumes it as sideband and sees the command count byte as the opcode.

retail_anchors.observed: Writes three longs with MSG_WriteLong, then writes one byte from sub_4AF4D0() xor serverCommandSequence low byte before reliable command opcodes.
Reads three longs with MSG_ReadLong, consumes one MSG_ReadByte sideband byte, then reads the first command opcode.

Source reconstruction helper now returns a persistent retail sideband flag byte initialized to 0x80, CL_Frame raises the recovered 0x20 viewangle-delta bit, R_PerformanceCounters feeds the recovered low-five renderer count, and CL_CheckCGameNativeImportIntegrity raises the recovered 0x40 import-guard bit.

resolved_conflict.previous_local_note: Earlier ledger/header notes treated committed SV_ExecuteClientMessage HLIL as three longs followed immediately by opcode parsing.
resolved_conflict.resolution: Committed retail HLIL actually shows an unassigned MSG_ReadByte after the third long and before opcode parsing. Source now writes and consumes that sideband byte.

residual_risks: The sideband producer map is captured in docs/reverse-engineering/network-client-message-sideband-producers-2026-06-05.json.
The source now emits all observed retail sideband producers; capture-backed packet comparison is still required before claiming byte-for-byte sideband completeness.
Full XOR byte-vector tests remain the next transport task.
